---

title: "D04_00: Product Dictionary Consolidation"
subtitle: "Create unified product dictionary from all product lines"
parent: "D04_poisson_marketing"
chapter: "CH12"
category: "derivation-task"
number: "D04_00"
date-created: "2025-12-14"
date-modified: "2025-12-30"
author: "Claude"
type: "derivation-task"
depends_on:
  - "D03_positioning_analysis"
outputs_to:
  - "D04_04_time_series"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
---

# D04_00: Product Dictionary Consolidation {#overview}

This task consolidates product profiles from all product lines into a unified dictionary for product_line_id mapping during sales data processing.

## Task Definition (NSQL)

```nsql
CONSOLIDATE raw_data.df_product_profile_{product_line_id}
TO processed_data.df_product_profile_dictionary
```

## Detailed Consolidation (NSQL)

```nsql
# D04_00: Product Dictionary Consolidation
CONSOLIDATE D04_00_product_dictionary:
  # Get all product line IDs from configuration
  product_line_ids: vec_product_line_id FROM app_config

  # Consolidate all product profiles
  FOR EACH product_line_id IN product_line_ids:
    UNION ALL:
      SELECT
        product_id
        product_line_id: product_line_id  # Add product line identifier
        product_name: COALESCE(name, product_id)
        brand
        category
        asin  # Amazon-specific
        sku   # General identifier
        created_date: CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
      FROM raw_data.df_product_profile_{product_line_id}

  # Deduplicate by product_id
  DEDUPLICATE BY product_id
  KEEP: FIRST (ORDER BY created_date DESC)

  # Validate dictionary completeness
  VALIDATE:
    no_null_product_id: product_id IS NOT NULL
    no_duplicate_ids: UNIQUE(product_id)

  OUTPUT TO processed_data.df_product_profile_dictionary
```

## Dictionary Structure

The product dictionary contains:
- `product_id`: Unique product identifier
- `product_line_id`: Associated product line
- `product_name`: Product display name
- `brand`: Product brand
- `category`: Product category
- Platform-specific identifiers (ASIN for Amazon, etc.)

## Dictionary Schema (NSQL)

```nsql
# D04_00: Product Dictionary Schema Definition
DEFINE SCHEMA df_product_profile_dictionary:
  COLUMNS:
    product_id: VARCHAR [PRIMARY_KEY, NOT_NULL]
    product_line_id: CHAR(3) [NOT_NULL, FOREIGN_KEY]
    product_name: VARCHAR
    brand: VARCHAR
    category: VARCHAR
    asin: VARCHAR(10) [NULLABLE]  # Amazon Standard Identification Number
    sku: VARCHAR [NULLABLE]       # Stock Keeping Unit
    url: VARCHAR [NULLABLE]
    created_date: TIMESTAMP [DEFAULT: CURRENT_TIMESTAMP]

  INDEXES:
    idx_product_line: ON (product_line_id)
    idx_asin: ON (asin) WHERE asin IS NOT NULL
    idx_brand: ON (brand)

  CONSTRAINTS:
    valid_asin: asin MATCHES '^[A-Z0-9]{10}$' OR asin IS NULL
```

## Implementation Status {#implementation}

There is **no standalone D04_00 DRV script** in MAMBA. Current behavior:

- **EBY**: Product profile tables are read directly in
  `scripts/update_scripts/ETL/eby/eby_ETL_sales_time_series_2TR___MAMBA.R`
  to build an in-memory dictionary for mapping.
- **CBZ**: Product line mapping is performed in
  `scripts/update_scripts/ETL/cbz/cbz_ETL_sales_time_series_2TR.R` using item
  profiles and the app product line list, so time series tables carry
  `product_line_id` in `app_data`.

This step is documented for conceptual completeness only.

## Output Schema

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `product_id` | VARCHAR | Unique product identifier |
| `product_line_id` | CHAR(3) | Associated product line |
| `product_name` | VARCHAR | Product display name |
| `brand` | VARCHAR | Product brand |
| `category` | VARCHAR | Product category |
| `asin` | VARCHAR(10) | Amazon Standard Identification Number |
| `sku` | VARCHAR | Stock Keeping Unit |
| `created_date` | TIMESTAMP | Record creation time |

## Key Principle

The product dictionary serves as the **single source of truth** for product_line_id mapping, enabling consistent filtering across all downstream derivation steps.

## Next Task

After dictionary creation (or in-memory mapping), proceed to:
- [D04_04: Time Series Expansion](D04_04_time_series.qmd) for EBY
- [D04_02: Poisson Analysis Core](D04_02_poisson_analysis.qmd) for CBZ/EBY
